title: mentha longifolia f. norica
intro: Mentha longifolia f. norica, also known as the Bulgarian mint or Morrocan mint, is a subspecies of the mint plant (Mentha longifolia) native to North Africa and the Balkans. It has been used for centuries for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Mentha longifolia f. norica include relieving digestive issues, reducing fever, and soothing headaches, as well as acting as an expectorant to help clear mucus from the lungs. In herbal preparations, the leaves of the Mentha longifolia f. norica plant are typically dried and used to make teas, infusions, and essential oils.
